revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Yokoana stone chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! == Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Yokoana stone chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! == Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Yokoana stone chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=draft}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=draft}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Yokoana stone chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=draft}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=draft}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=draft}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=draft}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺|12=draft}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=draft}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=draft}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Yokoana stone chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=draft}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=draft}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=draft}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=draft}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=draft}}

== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=draft}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=draft}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=draft}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Yokoana stone chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=draft}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=draft}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=draft}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=draft}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺|12=draft}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=draft}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! == Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster-->
<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> <!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> <!--Shijima Kofun Cluster-->
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofuns in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:City-designated historic sites in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shijima Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shijima Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:志島古墳群]] [[sn:hijima Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shijima Kofun Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shijima Kofun Cluster}} {{Archaeology}}

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=draft}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=draft}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Yokoana stone chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=draft}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=draft}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=draft}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=draft}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺|12=draft}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=draft}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label=志島古墳群|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|志島古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''志島古墳群'''（しじまこふんぐん）は、[[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島にある[[古墳群]]。1基が三重県指定史跡に、3基が志摩市指定史跡に指定され、11号墳出土の埴製枕が三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == 三重県東部、太平洋を見下ろす岬の丘陵台地上に築造された古墳群である。かつては13基以上が分布したが、現在は4基のみが遺存する{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。これまでに4号墳・11号墳で発掘調査が実施されている。 4基のうち最古の11号墳（[[おじょか古墳]]）は、[[5世紀]]中葉-後半頃の築造とされる。全国的にも古い[[横穴式石室]]を持ち、北部九州の石室形態である点、全国的にも珍しい埴製枕を有する点で注目される。10号墳（上村古墳）は[[6世紀]]後半頃の築造とされ、石室内に長大な箱式石棺を持ち、珍しい金糸・銅椀が出土している。4号墳（[[塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|塚穴古墳]]）は[[7世紀]]前半頃の築造とされ、[[高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|高倉山古墳]]（[[伊勢市]]）と同様の長大な玄室を有する。また古墳群全体として鏡の保有の多い点でも特色を示す{{Sfn|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}。 志島古墳群では11号墳の出現から次の古墳築造までの間に空白期間があるが、その間には南方の大王町域で[[前方後円墳]]2基（[[泊古墳]]・鳶ヶ巣1号墳）などが築造されたと推定される{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。これら志島古墳群・泊古墳・鳶ヶ巣1号墳は志摩地方を拠点とする広い海上交通ネットワークを有した有力豪族の墳墓群と想定され、文献上に見える[[島津国造]]との関連を指摘する説が挙げられている『志摩のあけぼの展 -考古学からみた志摩の歴史-』志摩市教育委員会、2010年、pp. 1319。。なお、当地北方では[[律令制]]下にも志摩国府・[[志摩国分寺]]が営まれたと推定されている{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 現存する4基のうち11号墳は[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に三重県指定史跡に、4・9・10号墳は[[1971年]]（昭和46年）に志摩市指定史跡に指定されている。また、11号墳出土の埴製枕は[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）に三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）、10号墳の墳丘・石室が大雨で半壊。副葬品出土{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 * [[昭和]]30年代、11号墳の石室開口。副葬品出土{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 * [[1967年]]（昭和42年）、11号墳の発掘調査（阿児町教育委員会、[[1968年]]に概報刊行）{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）3月28日、11号墳が三重県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1971年]]（昭和46年）3月31日、4・9・10号墳が「志島古墳群」として阿児町指定史跡に指定（現在は志摩市指定史跡）。 * [[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）3月13日、11号墳出土の埴製枕が三重県指定有形文化財に指定。 * [[2010年|2010]]-[[2012年]]度（平成22-24年度）、11号墳出土の金属製品の保存処理（志摩市教育委員会、[[2016年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * 2012-[[2014年]]度（平成24-26年度）、4号墳の発掘調査（志摩市教育委員会、[[2018年]]に概要報告）{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。 == 一覧 == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+志島古墳群の一覧{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}} !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !埋葬施設 !出土品 !築造時期 !史跡指定 !備考 |- |'''3号墳'''|| || || ||五鈴鏡|| || ||style="text-align:left"|消滅 |- （塚穴古墳）]]'''||[[円墳]]||直径18m||[[横穴式石室]]||[[須恵器]]||[[7世紀]]前半||志摩市指定史跡||style="text-align:left"|2012-2014年度発掘調査

装身具・鉄製品・[[埴輪]]・須恵器など||[[5世紀]]中葉||三重県指定史跡||style="text-align:left"|1967年発掘調査

出土埴製枕は三重県指定有形文化財 |- |'''12号墳'''|| || ||| ||仿製五獣鏡|| || ||style="text-align:left"|消滅 |} Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|[[塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|塚穴古墳]]（4号墳） Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-B.jpg|塚穴古墳 石室俯瞰図 Matsumotozuka Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|松本塚古墳（9号墳） Uemura Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|上村古墳（10号墳） 上村古墳 出土品.JPG|上村古墳 出土品 Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|[[おじょか古墳]]（11号墳） Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|おじょか古墳 石室俯瞰図 おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|おじょか古墳 埴製枕 == 文化財 == === 三重県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 埴製枕（考古資料） - おじょか古墳出土。志摩市歴史民俗資料館保管。1995年（平成7年）3月13日指定[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730977&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 埴製枕]（三重県教育委員会）。。 * 史跡 ** おじょか古墳 - 1969年（昭和44年）3月28日指定[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 おじょか古墳]（三重県教育委員会）。[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html 志摩市の文化財]（志摩市ホームページ）。。 === 志摩市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 志島古墳群 - 1971年（昭和46年）3月31日指定。 == 関連施設 == * [[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]（志摩市磯部町迫間） - 志島古墳群の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県史|volume=資料編 考古1|publisher=三重県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460|title=おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告 -金属製品編-|series=志摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告4|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=塚穴古墳（志島古墳群4号墳）発掘調査概報|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|series=志摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告5|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf 志摩市の文化財]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志摩市の文化財|2018}}}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]24|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490247|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=北村光比古|chapter=かくれた志摩の散歩道―史跡めぐり―|editor=三重地理学会|title=三重県の地理散歩|publisher=[[荘人社]]|date=1985-01-20|page=131-134|ISBN=4-915597-02-4|ref={{sfnref|北村|1985}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=下村登良男|year=1989|chapter=おじょか古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=伊藤保|title=王女丘古墳とその出土品について|date=1966|journal=郷土志摩|publisher=志摩郷土会|volume=|number=34|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1968|chapter=|title=志摩・おじょか古墳発掘調査概要 -三重県志摩郡阿児町大字志島-|publisher=阿児町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=村上喜雄|title=志摩の横穴式石室|date=1976|journal=郷土志摩|publisher=志摩郷土会|volume=|number=49|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1992|chapter=阿児町志島古墳群の調査|title=紀伊半島の文化史的研究|volume=考古学編|series=関西大学文学部考古学研究第6冊|publisher=清文堂出版|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=|title=志摩国の古墳|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]考古学研究会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=宮原佑治|title=志摩における後期古墳の研究(1) 志島上村古墳の研究|date=2016-03-30|journal=専修考古学|publisher=専修大学考古学会|volume=|number=15|pages=117-138|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/ojyokakohun.pdf おじょか古墳発掘50年記念シンポジウム「おじょか古墳と5世紀の倭」記録集]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Shijima Kofun Group}} * [[島津国造]] * [[泊古墳]] {{デフォルトソート:ししまこふんくん}} [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[志島古墳群]] to [[Shijima Kofun Cluster]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label=志島古墳群|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|志島古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''志島古墳群'''（しじまこふんぐん）は、[[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島にある[[古墳群]]。1基が三重県指定史跡に、3基が志摩市指定史跡に指定され、11号墳出土の埴製枕が三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == 三重県東部、太平洋を見下ろす岬の丘陵台地上に築造された古墳群である。かつては13基以上が分布したが、現在は4基のみが遺存する{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}}。これまでに4号墳・11号墳で発掘調査が実施されている。 4基のうち最古の11号墳（[[おじょか古墳]]）は、[[5世紀]]中葉-後半頃の築造とされる。全国的にも古い[[横穴式石室]]を持ち、北部九州の石室形態である点、全国的にも珍しい埴製枕を有する点で注目される。10号墳（上村古墳）は[[6世紀]]後半頃の築造とされ、石室内に長大な箱式石棺を持ち、珍しい金糸・銅椀が出土している。4号墳（[[塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|塚穴古墳]]）は[[7世紀]]前半頃の築造とされ、[[高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|高倉山古墳]]（[[伊勢市]]）と同様の長大な玄室を有する。また古墳群全体として鏡の保有の多い点でも特色を示す{{Sfn|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}。 志島古墳群では11号墳の出現から次の古墳築造までの間に空白期間があるが、その間には南方の大王町域で[[前方後円墳]]2基（[[泊古墳]]・鳶ヶ巣1号墳）などが築造されたと推定される{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。これら志島古墳群・泊古墳・鳶ヶ巣1号墳は志摩地方を拠点とする広い海上交通ネットワークを有した有力豪族の墳墓群と想定され、文献上に見える[[島津国造]]との関連を指摘する説が挙げられている『志摩のあけぼの展 -考古学からみた志摩の歴史-』志摩市教育委員会、2010年、pp. 1319。。なお、当地北方では[[律令制]]下にも志摩国府・[[志摩国分寺]]が営まれたと推定されている{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 現存する4基のうち11号墳は[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に三重県指定史跡に、4・9・10号墳は[[1971年]]（昭和46年）に志摩市指定史跡に指定されている。また、11号墳出土の埴製枕は[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）に三重県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）、10号墳の墳丘・石室が大雨で半壊。副葬品出土{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 * [[昭和]]30年代、11号墳の石室開口。副葬品出土{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 * [[1967年]]（昭和42年）、11号墳の発掘調査（阿児町教育委員会、[[1968年]]に概報刊行）{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）3月28日、11号墳が三重県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1971年]]（昭和46年）3月31日、4・9・10号墳が「志島古墳群」として阿児町指定史跡に指定（現在は志摩市指定史跡）。 * [[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）3月13日、11号墳出土の埴製枕が三重県指定有形文化財に指定。 * [[2010年|2010]]-[[2012年]]度（平成22-24年度）、11号墳出土の金属製品の保存処理（志摩市教育委員会、[[2016年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}。 * 2012-[[2014年]]度（平成24-26年度）、4号墳の発掘調査（志摩市教育委員会、[[2018年]]に概要報告）{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。 == 一覧 == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:80%" |+志島古墳群の一覧{{Sfn|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}{{Sfn|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}{{Sfn|志摩市の文化財|2018}} !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !埋葬施設 !出土品 !築造時期 !史跡指定 !備考 |- |'''3号墳'''|| || || ||五鈴鏡|| || ||style="text-align:left"|消滅 |- 装身具・鉄製品・[[埴輪]]・須恵器など||[[5世紀]]中葉||三重県指定史跡||style="text-align:left"|1967年発掘調査

出土埴製枕は三重県指定有形文化財 |- |'''12号墳'''|| || ||| ||仿製五獣鏡|| || ||style="text-align:left"|消滅 |} Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|[[塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|塚穴古墳]]（4号墳） Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-B.jpg|塚穴古墳 石室俯瞰図 Matsumotozuka Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|松本塚古墳（9号墳） Uemura Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|上村古墳（10号墳） 上村古墳 出土品.JPG|上村古墳 出土品 Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|[[おじょか古墳]]（11号墳） Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|おじょか古墳 石室俯瞰図 おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|おじょか古墳 埴製枕 == 文化財 == === 三重県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 埴製枕（考古資料） - おじょか古墳出土。志摩市歴史民俗資料館保管。1995年（平成7年）3月13日指定[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730977&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 埴製枕]（三重県教育委員会）。。 * 史跡 ** おじょか古墳 - 1969年（昭和44年）3月28日指定[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730505&pageCur=7&pub_id=2 おじょか古墳]（三重県教育委員会）。[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html 志摩市の文化財]（志摩市ホームページ）。。 === 志摩市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 志島古墳群 - 1971年（昭和46年）3月31日指定。 == 関連施設 == * [[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]（志摩市磯部町迫間） - 志島古墳群の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県史|volume=資料編 考古1|publisher=三重県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/16460|title=おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告 -金属製品編-|series=志摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告4|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|おじょか古墳（志島古墳群11号墳）発掘調査報告|2016}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=塚穴古墳（志島古墳群4号墳）発掘調査概報|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|series=志摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告5|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf 志摩市の文化財]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志摩市の文化財|2018}}}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]24|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490247|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=北村光比古|chapter=かくれた志摩の散歩道―史跡めぐり―|editor=三重地理学会|title=三重県の地理散歩|publisher=[[荘人社]]|date=1985-01-20|page=131-134|ISBN=4-915597-02-4|ref={{sfnref|北村|1985}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=下村登良男|year=1989|chapter=おじょか古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|おじょか古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=伊藤保|title=王女丘古墳とその出土品について|date=1966|journal=郷土志摩|publisher=志摩郷土会|volume=|number=34|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1968|chapter=|title=志摩・おじょか古墳発掘調査概要 -三重県志摩郡阿児町大字志島-|publisher=阿児町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=村上喜雄|title=志摩の横穴式石室|date=1976|journal=郷土志摩|publisher=志摩郷土会|volume=|number=49|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1992|chapter=阿児町志島古墳群の調査|title=紀伊半島の文化史的研究|volume=考古学編|series=関西大学文学部考古学研究第6冊|publisher=清文堂出版|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=|title=志摩国の古墳|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]考古学研究会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=宮原佑治|title=志摩における後期古墳の研究(1) 志島上村古墳の研究|date=2016-03-30|journal=専修考古学|publisher=専修大学考古学会|volume=|number=15|pages=117-138|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/ojyokakohun.pdf おじょか古墳発掘50年記念シンポジウム「おじょか古墳と5世紀の倭」記録集]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Shijima Kofun Group}} * [[島津国造]] * [[泊古墳]] {{デフォルトソート:ししまこふんくん}} [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Yokoana stone chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.

* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofuns in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:City-designated historic sites in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shijima Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] }}
[[sn:Shijima Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:志島古墳群]] [[sn:hijima Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shijima Kofun Cluster]]

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Yokoana stone chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofuns in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[Category:City-designated historic sites in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shijima Kofun Cluster]]

[[sn:Shijima Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:志島古墳群]] [[sn:hijima Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shijima Kofun Cluster]]
<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster-->

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Yokoana stone chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! == Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Shijima Kofun Cluster]]
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima, Mie|qq=}}, [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Provincial Temple|ja|志摩国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Provincial Temple|qq=}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717}}

=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}}

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
