[[sn:Shijima Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:志島古墳群]] [[sn:hijima Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shijima Kofun Cluster]]
<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}}

<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}}

The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie�]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shijima Kofun Group}}

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|志島古墳群|version=103793533|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shijima Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. === Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[2010]]–[[2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! <!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shijima Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717}}

== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! <!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shijima Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[:en:2014|2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[:en:2018|2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[:en:2014|2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[:en:2018|2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Ojoka Kofun|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}}

<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shijima Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Mie Prefecture]]" to "[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]"
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] and features a long box-style stone coffin. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[:en:2014|2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[:en:2018|2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[Sue pottery]] || Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.
