<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q85880289]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|1=Shijima Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880289}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[:en:2014|2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[:en:2018|2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q85880289]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|1=Shijima Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880289}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[:en:2014|2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[:en:2018|2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] || Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q85880289]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|1=Shijima Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880289}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[:en:2014|2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[:en:2018|2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] || Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[:en:2014|2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[:en:2018|2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shijima Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q85880289}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85880289]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|1=Shijima Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880289}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[:en:2014|2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[:en:2018|2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85880289]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|1=Shijima Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880289}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and [[:en:Shima Kokubun-ji|Shima Provincial Temple]] to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* [[:en:1916|1916]]: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1967|1967]]: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in [[:en:1968|1968]]{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * [[:en:1969|1969]]: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1971|1971]]: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:1995|1995]]: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * [[:en:2010|2010]]–[[:en:2012|2012]]: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * [[:en:2012|2012]]–[[:en:2014|2014]]: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in [[:en:2018|2018]]{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] || Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid [[:en:5th century|5th century]] || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}}

One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Kokubun-ji|en|Shima Kokubun-ji|WD=Q67619852|lt=Shima Provincial Temple}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}}: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}}: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969}}: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971}}: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}–{{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}}: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}}–{{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014}}: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018}}{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} || Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>

During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Kokubun-ji|en|Shima Kokubun-ji|WD=Q67619852|lt=Shima Provincial Temple}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}}: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}}: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969}}: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971}}: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}–{{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}}: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}}–{{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014}}: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018}}{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shijima Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q85880289}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85880289]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|1=Shijima Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880289}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Kokubun-ji|en|Shima Kokubun-ji|WD=Q67619852|lt=Shima Provincial Temple}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}}: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}}: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969}}: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971}}: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}–{{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}}: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}}–{{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014}}: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018}}{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.

Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Kokubun-ji|en|Shima Kokubun-ji|WD=Q67619852|lt=Shima Provincial Temple}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009}}}} {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|1=Shijima Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880289}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Kokubun-ji|en|Shima Kokubun-ji|WD=Q67619852|lt=Shima Provincial Temple}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}}: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967}}: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969}}: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971}}: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}}–{{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}}: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012}}–{{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014}}: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018}}{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! Name !! Type !! Size !! Burial Facility !! Artifacts !! Date !! Historic Site !! Notes |- | '''Mound 3''' || || || || Five-ridge mirror || || || Destroyed |- | '''{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4)''' || [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] || 18m diameter || [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style burial chamber]]|| {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} || Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} || Shima City Historic Site || Excavated 2012–2014 |- | '''Matsumotozuka Kofun (Mound 9)''' || Round mound || 14m diameter || Unknown || || || Shima City Historic Site || Unexcavated |- | '''{{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Mound 10)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber with box-style coffin || Mirrors, gold threads, copper bowl, adornments, weapons || Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} || Shima City Historic Site || Partial collapse in 1916 |- | '''{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11)''' || Unknown || Unknown || Yokoana-style burial chamber || Clay pillow, mirrors, adornments, Sue pottery || Mid {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} || Prefectural Historic Site || Excavated 1967 |- | '''Mound 12''' || || || || Imitation five-beast mirror || || || Destroyed |} <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|{{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 4) Ojoka Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|{{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}} (Mound 11) Ojoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Ojoka Kofun: Burial Chamber おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Ojoka Kofun: Clay Pillow </gallery>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Clay pillow (archaeological artifact)''' – Excavated from Ojoka Kofun. Stored at Shima Historical and Folklore Museum. Designated March 13, 1995<ref name="mieken"/>.
=== Shima City Historic Sites === * '''Shijima Kofuns''' – Designated March 31, 1971<ref name="list"/>.
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}} – Houses artifacts from the Shijima Kofuns.

<!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--><!--Shijima Kofun Cluster--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shijima Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q85880289}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009|1=Shima, Mie}}}} {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|1=Shijima Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880289}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima, Mie|1=Shima, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Kokubun-ji|en|Shima Kokubun-ji|WD=Q67619852|lt=Shima Provincial Temple|1=Shima Kokubun-ji}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}}: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967|1=1967}}: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969|1=1969}}: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}}: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}}–{{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}–{{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}}: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}}{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.

=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Kokubun-ji|en|Shima Kokubun-ji|WD=Q67619852|lt=Shima Provincial Temple|1=Shima Kokubun-ji}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}}: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967|1=1967}}: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969|1=1969}}: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}}: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}}–{{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}–{{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}}: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}}{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} ! == Cultural Properties ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shijima Kofun Group]][[ja:志島古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:志島古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009|1=Shima, Mie}}}} {{Location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=18|lat_sec=39.68|lon_deg=136|lon_min=53|lon_sec=20.03|label={{ill|Shijima Kofuns|ja|志島古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijima Kofuns|1=Shijima Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880289}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Shijima Kofuns}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Shijima Kofuns''' (志島古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima, Mie|1=Shima, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. One mound is designated as a [[Prefectural Historic Site of Japan]], three are designated as {{ill|Shima City Historic Sites|ja|志摩市指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historic Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a clay pillow excavated from the 11th mound is designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property.
== Overview ==
The Kofuns are situated on a hilltop plateau overlooking the Pacific Ocean in eastern Mie Prefecture. While over 13 mounds originally existed, only four remain today{{Sfn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}}. Excavations have been conducted at the 4th and 11th mounds.
=== Mound 11 (Ojoka Kofun) === The 11th mound, also known as the {{ill|Ojoka Kofun|ja|おじょか古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojoka Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the mid-to-late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. It is notable for its early example of a [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|yokoana-style burial chamber]] (横穴式石室), which exhibits characteristics of northern Kyushu burial architecture. It also contained a rare clay pillow, drawing significant attention.
=== Mound 10 (Uemura Kofun) === The 10th mound, or {{ill|Uemura Kofun|ja|上村古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uemura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} and features a long box-style Sarcophagus. Notable artifacts include gold threads, a copper bowl, personal adornments, and iron weapons.
=== Mound 4 (Tsukaana Kofun) === The 4th mound, also known as the {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (志摩市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun|qq=}}, dates to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} and features an exceptionally long burial chamber similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|lt=Takakurayama Kofun|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}.
=== General Features === These mounds are characterized by a high number of mirrors among the grave goods, which is a notable feature of the site{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1983}}.
The interval between the 11th mound and subsequent mounds suggests a pause in mound construction. During this gap, keyhole-shaped Kofuns such as the {{ill|Tomari Kofun|ja|泊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomari Kofun|qq=}} and the Tobi-ga-Su Mound 1 were likely constructed in the nearby Daio Town area{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. The Shijima Kofuns, along with these sites, are believed to belong to a powerful clan that controlled a wide maritime network. Some researchers associate these Kofuns with the {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Dawn of Shima: Archaeological Perspectives on Shima History|publisher=Shima City Education Board|year=2010|pages=13–19}}</ref>. Evidence suggests the presence of the [[Shima Provincial Capital]] and {{ill|Shima Kokubun-ji|en|Shima Kokubun-ji|WD=Q67619852|lt=Shima Provincial Temple|1=Shima Kokubun-ji}} to the north during the [[Ritsuryō system]] period{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}.
== Historical Timeline ==
* {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}}: Heavy rain caused partial collapse of Mound 10, exposing grave goods{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1967|en|1967|WD=Q2648|lt=1967|1=1967}}: Excavation of Mound 11 by the {{ill|Ago Town Education Board|ja|阿児町教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ago Town Education Board|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with a preliminary report published in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}. * {{ill|1969|en|1969|WD=Q2485|lt=1969|1=1969}}: Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Historic Site<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1971|en|1971|WD=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}}: Mounds 4, 9, and 10 designated as Shima City Historic Sites<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}: Clay pillow excavated from Mound 11 designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property<ref name="list"/>. * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}}–{{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}: Conservation of metal artifacts from Mound 11{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}. * {{ill|2012|en|2012|WD=Q1990|lt=2012|1=2012}}–{{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}}: Excavation of Mound 4, with a summary report published in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}}{{Sfn|Excavation Reports of Shima Archaeological Sites (2018)|2018}}.
== List of Mounds == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |+List of Shijima Kofuns{{Sfn|Shijima Kofuns (Mie Prefectural History)|2005}}{{Sfn|Ojoka Kofun Excavation Report|2016}}{{SFn|Shima City Cultural Properties|2018}} !
